v1_value: Attempt to reach dispatch line (monitored by Linda until 8 PM)
If plumbing emergency, attempt to reach on-call plumber Jake
If HVAC emergency, attempt to reach HVAC lead Steve
If no response from Jake or Steve, attempt to reach owner Tom
v2_value: For plumbing emergencies (burst pipes, water leaks, sewer, frozen pipes), attempt to transfer to Jake (on-call plumber) at 303-555-0134 for 30 seconds.
For HVAC emergencies (no heat, AC failure in critical areas, boiler issues), attempt to transfer to Steve (HVAC lead) at 303-555-0156 for 30 seconds.

For gas leaks, Clara must immediately instruct the caller to evacuate the building, not flip any light switches, not use the phone inside, get outside, and call 911 from outside FIRST. After this safety message, Clara should attempt to transfer to Tom Chen (Owner) at 303-555-0112.
If Jake or Steve do not answer within 30 seconds, escalate to Tom Chen (Owner) at 303-555-0112 as backup for all emergencies.
If Tom Chen does not answer after all attempts, take a detailed message.

changes.v2_value: If no one answers after all attempts, take a detailed message including the exact issue, property address, and property manager contact (if known). Send text alerts to Tom Chen, Linda Chen, and the relevant on-call technician (Jake or Steve depending on the trade).

changes.v1_value: Clara to answer calls, qualify intent, and provide general information. For non-emergency service requests, Clara will collect necessary information and inform the caller that someone will call them back during business hours.
changes.v2_value: Clara is to collect specific information from the caller.

v1_value: Integration with FieldEdge for dispatching and job tracking.

changes.v1_value: During after-hours (weekends and Monday-Friday after 5:30 PM Mountain Time), Clara will identify if the call is an emergency based on predefined triggers (burst pipes, major water leaks, sewer backups, gas leaks, no heat in winter below freezing, complete loss of hot water at large facility, HVAC failures in server rooms/medical facilities). If it's an emergency, the call will first attempt to reach the dispatch line (monitored by Linda until 8 PM). After 8 PM, or if the dispatch line is unresponsive, plumbing emergencies will be routed to Jake at 303-555-0134, and HVAC emergencies to Steve at 303-555-0156. If neither Jake nor Steve responds, the call will escalate to Tom, the owner, at 303-555-0112. Non-emergency calls after hours will be handled by Clara collecting information for a callback during business hours.
changes.v2_value: During after-hours (outside Mon-Fri, 7 AM-5:30 PM MT, and after 8 PM on business days, or all day weekends), Clara will first determine if the call is an emergency. If it's a gas leak, Clara will immediately deliver a critical safety message (evacuate, call 911) before attempting to transfer to Tom Chen. For other emergencies, Clara will follow the conditional emergency routing rules, attempting to reach Jake for plumbing issues or Steve for HVAC issues, with Tom Chen as the final escalation. If no one answers, Clara will take a detailed message and send text alerts to Tom, Linda, and the relevant on-call tech. For non-emergency calls after hours, Clara will collect the caller's name, phone number, company name (if commercial), property address, type of issue, and urgency, informing them that Evergreen Mechanical will call back the next business morning. On weekends, Linda Chen is skipped entirely for all calls, going straight to on-call techs and Tom for emergencies.

changes.v1_value: During business hours (Monday-Friday, 7 AM to 5:30 PM Mountain Time), Clara will answer incoming calls, qualify the caller's intent, and determine if it's an emergency or a routine service request. For routine inquiries, Clara will collect necessary information (name, phone number, nature of issue) for the office staff (Linda, Rachel, Amy) to follow up. Clara will ensure not to promise specific arrival times for technicians, instead using phrases like 'we'll get someone out as soon as possible'. The goal is to reduce the call volume handled by the office staff, allowing them to focus on dispatching and billing.
changes.v2_value: During office hours (Monday through Friday, 7 AM to 5:30 PM Mountain Time), Clara will primarily attempt to transfer all incoming calls to Linda Chen (Office Manager) at 303-555-0189. If Linda does not answer within 20 seconds, Clara will take a message, and Linda will commit to calling back within 30 minutes. For clients specifically requesting Tom Chen, Clara will attempt to transfer to his cell at 303-555-0112. For billing-related inquiries, Clara will take a message for Amy in accounting. If an emergency call comes in during office hours, Clara will follow the same emergency routing rules as after-hours, attempting to reach the appropriate on-call technician (Jake or Steve) or Tom Chen for gas leaks, with Tom as the general backup.

v1_value: AI agent must not promise specific arrival times for technicians; instead, use phrases like 'we'll get someone out as soon as possible'.
Emergency routing is separated by trade (plumbing vs. HVAC).
Weekends are closed except for emergencies.
v2_value: Clara must never promise a specific arrival time for technicians. Instead, use phrases like 'we'll dispatch someone as soon as possible'.

For calls reporting a gas smell, Clara must immediately interrupt the call to deliver a critical safety message: 'Leave the building, don't flip any light switches, don't use the phone inside, get outside, call 911 from outside, and THEN we can help.' This is a priority override.
Do not configure Saturday hours at this time, as they are not yet implemented by Evergreen Mechanical Services.

changes.v1_value: Evergreen Mechanical Services is a full-service mechanical contractor based in Denver, Colorado, specializing in plumbing, HVAC, and light electrical for commercial properties (apartment complexes, office buildings, retail spaces). They have been in business for 20 years. The company currently experiences overwhelming call volumes (60-70 calls/day during busy season) and struggles with after-hours calls using an old answering service that lacks qualification capabilities. The corporate structure includes Tom (Owner), Linda (Office Manager), and two other office staff members, Rachel and Amy. They utilize FieldEdge for dispatching and job tracking. A specific 'dispatch line' is mentioned, which Linda monitors until 8 PM for emergencies. There was no mention of Jobber or QuickBooks usage. The AI solution, Clara, is intended to alleviate call volume and improve after-hours emergency handling. Pricing for the Clara software was not discussed in this transcript. An onboarding call will be scheduled following agreement.
changes.v2_value: Updated with onboarding data. Operational details confirmed.
